title: Obtain the String
description: You are given two strings s and t. In one move, you can select any character of s and delete it. Determine the minimum number of moves needed to make t a subsequence of s.
input_spec: The first line contains string s (1 <= |s| <= 10^5). The second line contains string t (1 <= |t| <= 10^5).
output_spec: Print the minimum number of deletions, or -1 if it's impossible.

routes.name: Greedy subsequence matching
routes.description: Use two pointers to greedily match characters of t in s.

observations.title: Subsequence check via two pointers
observations.description: Walk through s with pointer i and t with pointer j. When s[i] == t[j], advance both; otherwise advance only i.

hints.nudge: How do you check if one string is a subsequence of another?
hints.question: If you scan s from left to right, how do you greedily match characters of t?
hints.formal: Use two pointers: i for s, j for t. If s[i] == t[j], increment both. Otherwise increment only i. t is a subsequence if j reaches |t|.

observations.title: Impossible case: character missing
observations.description: If t contains a character that doesn't appear in s at all, the answer is -1.

hints.nudge: When is it impossible to make t a subsequence of s, no matter what?
hints.question: What if t has a character 'z' but s doesn't contain 'z' at all?
hints.formal: Check: for every character in t, it must exist in s. If any character of t is absent from s, return -1.

observations.title: Deletions = |s| - matched
observations.description: After matching, the minimum deletions = number of characters in s that weren't part of the subsequence match.

hints.nudge: After matching t as a subsequence, what does deleting the extra characters in s accomplish?
hints.question: If you matched all of t using some characters of s, how many characters of s are 'extra'?
hints.formal: If j reached |t| (full match), the answer is 0 (t is already a subsequence). The minimum deletions to make t a subsequence is always 0 if t is already a subsequence, or -1 if it's impossible.
